title: HTTP/1.1 Message Framing and Length Precedence · Failure Repair Bay
summary: An incident engineer receives a plausible but defective RFC 9112 message-body length decision table; retain the raw transcript, isolate the first divergent state, and preserve a regression corpus entry. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in RFC 9112 message-body length decision table and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply the RFC 9112 message-body length precedence in order using request or response role, method, status, transfer coding, content length, and connection closure.
Forbid content framing for HEAD responses, 1xx, 204, successful CONNECT, and other context-specific cases while retaining metadata meaning.
Validate transfer-coding order, require final chunked coding for request framing, and close after ambiguous Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length input.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Order the regression proof · Debug Framing Completeness Evidence
steps.instruction:
Order the Framing Completeness Evidence evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Order the regression proof · Debug Framing Completeness Evidence.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Framing Completeness Evidence assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Order the regression proof · Debug Framing Completeness Evidence, keep Framing Completeness Evidence connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.text: A debug protocol team handles case 2509 in a protocol upgrade gateway transferring buffered tunnel bytes. Build deterministic Go evidence to apply framing precedence and close on Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length ambiguity; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then change certificate identity while ALPN stays valid and reject the connection. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record framing source, expected bytes, observed bytes, completion, residual buffer, close requirement, and rejection reason.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Framing Completeness Evidence has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

steps.title: Content Length List Validation · fault isolation · Debug Content Length List Validation
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug protocol team handles case 2514 in a regional status service with slow assistive and low-bandwidth clients. Build deterministic Go evidence to apply framing precedence and close on Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length ambiguity; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then change certificate identity while ALPN stays valid and reject the connection. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Parse decimal Content-Length values without overflow and accept repeated or list values only when every valid value is identical.

Which observation would disprove this suspected cause: “Selecting the first Content-Length value is safe when later values differ.”?

steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Framing Completeness Evidence, keep Framing Completeness Evidence connected to executable Go behavior evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Record framing source, expected bytes, observed bytes, completion, residual buffer, close requirement, and rejection reason.

hints: For Publish the incident record · Debug Framing Completeness Evidence, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Framing Completeness Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Publish the incident record · Debug Framing Completeness Evidence, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A debug protocol team handles case 2517 in a canary edge service advertising HTTP/3 with bounded fallback. Build deterministic Go evidence to apply framing precedence and close on Transfer-Encoding plus Content-Length ambiguity; preserve this operating constraint: one connection has exactly one read owner and one terminal close owner; then saturate one stream window while another remains independently serviceable. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Record framing source, expected bytes, observed bytes, completion, residual buffer, close requirement, and rejection reason.

For Publish the incident record · Debug Framing Completeness Evidence, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A valid start line and field section prove the complete message is safe to dispatch.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.
